title: Daily Station Check duplicate cron causing nightly token burn
summary: Cron job dae7406b (Daily Station Check) was an agentTurn job that burned tokens every night at 23:00 BST from May 21–31. It was supposed to be disabled when the shell-only LaunchAgent replacement (com.openclaw.daily-station-check) was created, but it remained enabled. Each run spawned an isolated AI session that hit the 120s timeout in model-call-started phase, costing tokens with zero useful output. 4 consecutive errors recorded. Root cause: old cron was never deleted when LaunchAgent was deployed. Fixed by permanently deleting cron job dae7406b on 2026-06-01.

timeline.message: First recorded failure of dae7406b — agentTurn timeout in model-call-started. LaunchAgent replacement already existed but old cron was never deleted.
Andre reports Daily Station Check has been 'fixed' three times and still burning tokens. Investigation launched.
Root cause confirmed: dae7406b was enabled the entire time alongside the LaunchAgent. Cron job permanently deleted. Added FRAMEWORK.md gotcha: always DELETE old cron when replacing with LaunchAgent.

actions.label: Delete old cron job dae7406b
actions.description: Permanently delete the agentTurn cron, not just disable it

actions.label: Add FRAMEWORK.md gotcha
actions.description: Document the rule: DELETE old cron when replacing with LaunchAgent

actions.label: Verify LaunchAgent is sole mechanism
actions.description: Confirm com.openclaw.daily-station-check is the only daily check mechanism and it's shell-only

title: Cron job failures — 8 active errors detected
summary: cron-errors.json shows 8 active error entries. Multiple cron jobs are failing.

title: Operational consolidation — cron jobs replaced with LaunchAgent maintenance script
summary: Replaced three high-frequency cron jobs (435 sessions/day) with single LaunchAgent running maintenance.sh every 30 minutes. All operational checks (MC health, error watchdog, task dispatcher, incident detection, session cleanup, backups) now run as shell-only with zero AI session overhead. Estimated savings: ~1.3M tokens/day.

title: WD MyCloud mount path changed — /Volumes/Public-1 regenerated after purge
summary: After gateway session purge, WD MyCloud mount appeared as /Volumes/Public-1 instead of /Volumes/Public. Scripts referencing the original path failed. mount-check and backup scripts need to handle both paths or prefer mount label over path.

title: Rate limit exhaustion (2nd wave) — free model pool depleted by session explosion
summary: Session explosion from runaway cron jobs created dozens of simultaneous session contexts, each consuming free-model rate limit quota. All free models returned 429. Resolved when cron jobs were terminated and sessions purged.

title: Session explosion — 22 to 87 sessions from runaway cron jobs
summary: Three cron jobs (Error Spike Watchdog every 5min, MC Health Check every 15min, Task Dispatcher every 30min) each created isolated sessions on every run. Combined rate of ~435 sessions/day with no session cleanup. Burned through token limits within hours. Fixed by deleting all three cron jobs and replacing with single LaunchAgent maintenance script (shell-only, zero sessions).

title: Gateway crash loop — EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError across all sessions
summary: All active sessions hit EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError simultaneously around 15:15 UTC May 21. Gateway was in a crash loop where session files were being modified during embedded prompt lock release. Required gateway restart and session purge.

title: Gateway update failure — missing modules after partial update to v2026.5.19
summary: Partial gateway update to v2026.5.19 left missing module dependencies. Gateway failed to start after update. Required manual intervention to restore.

title: Mission Control down — LaunchAgent pointing to deleted Wrangler binary
summary: The LaunchAgent com.openclaw.mission-control was pointing to a Wrangler binary in ~/Downloads/agent-control-station-main which no longer exists. Exit code 78 (EX_CONFIG) accumulated 9,473 restart attempts. The current Vite process was an unmanaged orphan.

title: Rate limit exhaustion — all models returned 429 starting May 15 19:42
summary: Starting 2026-05-15 19:42, owl-alpha began failing with all_keys_exhausted errors. The watcher log shows continuous rotation failures every ~16 minutes through 2026-05-16 00:49 (17+ failures). All API keys were exhausted, leaving the agent unable to process requests for several hours.

title: Cron job failures — Git Auto-Commit timeout and Activity Summary failing
summary: Multiple cron jobs have been failing: Git Auto-Commit timeout, Activity Summary fail. The cron-errors.json shows 6 active error entries. Daily station check at 23:00 has been impacted.

title: WD MyCloud mount failing — /Volumes/Public-1 not found (persistent since May 16 06:18)
summary: The mount-check.log shows continuous /Volumes/Public-1 not found errors every 30 minutes since at least 2026-05-16 06:18 (20+ errors). Backup scripts depending on this mount path are failing silently.

title: Tasks stuck In Progress — 3 tasks with no assignee or recent activity
summary: Three tasks (Error Feed behaviour review, Task Completion summary, Agent Office Overall) have been in In Progress state with no assigned owner. No activity updates visible. Tasks may be orphaned from agent assignments.

title: Build error — AnimatePresence SSR failure in Dashboard component
summary: ReferenceError: AnimatePresence is not defined at StationChatter in Dashboard.tsx. The awaitingReviewCount variable was defined inside QuickActionsPanel but referenced in the parent Dashboard component — a scoping bug causing SSR crash on the /visual page.

title: Port conflict — Mission Control dev server jumping between 3000 and 3001
summary: When the LaunchAgent restarts the dev server, it sometimes finds port 3000 still occupied by the previous process and falls back to 3001. This causes confusion about which port is serving the live app and can result in stale builds being served.

title: Disk space warning — Data volume at 85% (175 Gi / 228 Gi)
summary: Health log from 2026-05-15 12:03 shows /System/Volumes/Data at 85% usage (175 Gi / 228 Gi). Swap/compressor usage notable at ~21.9 GB. Approaching threshold where system performance may degrade.

title: Memory index corruption after session restart
summary: After an unplanned session restart, the SQLite memory index became partially corrupted. Daily log entries from 2026-05-11 onwards were not searchable. The raw .md files are intact — only the index needs rebuilding.

timeline.message: Incident auto-detected: memory search returning stale results
Confirmed SQLite index mismatch — 14 entries missing from index
Status escalated to MITIGATING, assigned to Archivist
Raw .md files verified intact, index rebuild initiated

title: WD MyCloud SMB mount permissions broke backups
summary: The WD MyCloud SMB share was mounted as read-only for the openclaw-agent account, preventing all backup scripts from running. The backup folder structure existed on the share but was completely empty.

timeline.message: Backup script failed: permission denied writing to /Volumes/WD-MyCloud/OpenClaw/logs/
Investigated mount permissions — volume owned by root:wheel, read-only for agent
Incident opened, assigned to Life Support for resolution tracking
New SMB share mounted at /Volumes/OpenClaw-WD with full read/write access
First successful backup completed to new mount path

actions.label: Remount share with write access
actions.description: Mount the WD MyCloud SMB share with user credentials for r/w

actions.label: Run first backup
actions.description: Execute full backup to verify write access works end-to-end

actions.label: Set up automated daily backups
actions.description: Configure cron job for daily 3am backup runs

title: Mission Control dashboard can't read zip from Desktop
summary: macOS security permissions prevent Terminal/Node from reading mission-control.zip on the Desktop. System shows a security prompt asking for Desktop folder access, but TTY-based processes can't interact with the GUI prompt.

timeline.message: First attempt to unzip mission-control.zip failed with EACCES
macOS TCC prompt appeared but can't be accepted by TTY process
Incident opened — needs human to either move file or grant Full Disk Access

actions.label: Move zip to ~/Downloads/
actions.description: Human moves mission-control.zip to Downloads folder where Node has access

actions.label: Grant Full Disk Access
actions.description: Add Terminal.app to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access

actions.label: Alternative: use open dialog
actions.description: Use macOS native file picker to grant one-time access to the zip

title: Cron job drift — daily heartbeat running 3 hours late
summary: The daily heartbeat cron job was scheduled for 03:00 but is consistently firing around 06:00. The gateway sleep/wake cycle is shifting the effective trigger time. This causes checks to run during active hours instead of quiet hours.

timeline.message: Daily heartbeat fired three hours late — gap analysis initiated
Root cause: gateway sleep shifts cron anchorMs forward on each resume
Proposed fix: switch to cron expr with tz instead of everyMs

actions.label: Switch to cron expression
actions.description: Replace everyMs schedule with cron '0 3 * * *' in Europe/London tz

actions.label: Add stagger tolerance
actions.description: Set staggerMs to 60000 to prevent exact-time clustering

actions.label: Monitor for 48h
actions.description: Verify the fix holds over two sleep/wake cycles
